rationale: The cited paper is used only once as part of a grouped background citation in the General Discussion, where the authors list prior evidence that promotions can have negative side effects (e.g., undermining perceived product efficacy). The citing paper does not rely on this source to build its hypotheses, operationalize constructs, design studies, or interpret its core mechanism tests. Its main theoretical development instead centers on reward-cue and impatience work, so this citation functions as contextual literature support rather than a substantive dependency.
